mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.0% fall below the 10-day moving average of baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates expanding real economic activity, which drives equity risk premiums higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods where INDPRO > 110 and HOUST > 1000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP growth below 2.0% signals deteriorating demand conditions that trigger risk-off sentiment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 2.0% exceed the 30-day moving average of baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between strong payroll growth and rising unemployment indicates labor market fragility, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE > 5.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts above 1000 units signals improving consumer confidence and demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and elevated policy rates signals a monetary tightening cycle that increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
